creator: Alexander Terp
description:
When formatting a float with alignment but no explicit precision or thousands separator, the output gets 6 trailing decimal places (Go default %f behavior).

**Repro:**
```
print("{3.14:>10}")    // "  3.140000" (expected "      3.14")
print("{42.0:<10}")    // "42.000000 " (expected "42        ")
```

**Workarounds that work correctly:**
```
print("{3.14:>10.2}")  // "      3.14" (explicit precision)
print("{3.14:>10,}")   // "      3.14" (thousands separator)
```

The bug triggers ONLY with alignment (< or >) AND no thousands separator AND no explicit precision. Root cause is likely falling through to Go's default %f formatting in the alignment-only code path.

**Test files:** `bugs/strings/str_format_float_precision_bug.rad`, `str_format_float_bug_precise.rad`
title: Float alignment without precision defaults to 6 decimal places
